Article Title: India sends medical assistance to Kenya to strengthen preparedness for Ebola
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Indian High Commissioner to Kenya Adarsh Swaika Tuesday handed over the first tranche of medical assistance, weighing 4.5 tonnes, to the country’s cabinet secretary for health Aden Duale at Nairobi’s Jomo Kenyatta International Airport. The medical assistance is intended to strengthen Kenya's long-term preparedness and response capacity for Ebola Virus Disease, other infectious diseases and future pandemic threats.The first tranche comprises 279 packages of essential medical supplies, including personal protective equipment (PPE) kits, sample transportation kits, infrared thermometers, face shields, heavy-duty protective boots and other infection-prevention materials. The second tranche of medical assistance will be sent soon.“The assistance is envisaged not merely as a short-term intervention, but to strengthen resilient and sustained public health preparedness. It will support the Kenya National Public Health Institute and other frontline public health institutions by strengthening capacities for disease surveillance, safe collection and transportation of samples, infection prevention and control, and protection of frontline healthcare personnel, thereby enhancing Kenya’s readiness to detect, contain and respond effectively to Ebola Virus Disease, other emerging infectious diseases and future pandemics,” the High Commission of India in Nairobi said in a statement.“This assistance follows medical supplies of over 45 tonnes to the Africa Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) in late May and early June 2026, to support the continent’s preparedness and response to Ebola Virus Disease. It reflects India’s broader commitment to strengthening public health and pandemic preparedness across Africa,” it added.India has been one of the first responders in Kenya during public health challenges. During the COVID-19 pandemic, India supplied 1.12 million doses of Made in India’ vaccines to Kenya under its Vaccine Maitri initiative, in addition to providing essential medicines and other medical assistance. The strong health partnership between the two countries extends beyond emergency support. In recent years, India has provided, on grant basis,a Bhabhatron radiotherapy machine to Kenyatta National Hospital for enhancing its cancer treatment capacity, a CT scan machine and an MRI machine (under process) to the Kenya Navy Hospital in Mombasa to further strengthen its healthcare infrastructure. Every year, more than 10,000 Kenyans travel to India for medical treatment, drawn by India's affordable, accessible and high-quality healthcare services. India is also Kenya's leading pharmaceutical partner, with Indian pharmaceutical products accounting for over 50% of Kenya's pharmaceutical market, ensuring the availability of quality and affordable medicines across the country,” it added.India and Kenya enjoy a longstanding partnership in the health sector, encompassing the supply of medicines and medical equipment, capacity building and training, affordable healthcare, and cooperation between their respective health institutions. The assistance reflects India’s people-centric development partnership with Kenya and its commitment to supporting partner countries in Africa in building stronger and more resilient health systems.
Article Summary: The medical assistance is intended to strengthen Kenya's long-term preparedness and response capacity for Ebola Virus Disease, other infectious diseases and future pandemic threats. The first tranche comprises 279 packages of essential medical supplies, including personal protective equipment (PPE) kits. The second tranche of medical assistance will be sent soon.

Article Title: 'Name is Nothing': CJP reacts to PM Modi's vow of punishing paper leak offenders with Dharmendra Pradhan's photo
Article Text:
CJP mocks PM Modi's paper leak action plan with Pradhan meme (Picture from X)

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke on Thursday reacted to Prime Minister Narendra Modi 's announcement of fast-track courts and stricter action over the NEET paper leak row, taking a satirical swipe at the Centre for not addressing the students' demand for education minister Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation.In a post on X, Dipke shared a picture of Dharmendra Pradhan with the caption: "Hi, my name is Nothing."The post was a reaction to Prime Minister's statement on X, which began with: "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!""We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps in this regard. This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students," PM Modi added. "Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," he said.The PM's assurance comes amidst widespread protests in Delhi and other cities.The internet-sensation-turned pressure group CJP has continued its protest at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar for over a month. Meanwhile, scientist and educationist Sonam Wangchuk, who is under medical supervision at Medanta Hospital, entered the 26th day of his hunger strike on Thursday.CJP spokesperson Sourav Das put forward their demands. "First, the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan. Second, 1 crore rupees compensation for the families of all those students who committed suicide due to the NEET paper leak. Third, all FIRs filed unnecessarily against peaceful protesters should be withdrawn, and we want a sovereign guarantee from the government that no such FIRs will be filed against any peaceful protester in relation to this protest in the future," he added.Das termed the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan as 'non-negotiable', adding that the protest would continue until the demand was met.
Article Summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke mocks PM Modi's paper leak action plan. Dipke shared a picture of Dharmendra Pradhan with the caption: "Hi, my name is Nothing" CJP has continued its protest at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar for over a month.

Article Title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
Article Text:
India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Parvathaneni Harish (PTI file photo)

NEW DELHI: India on Wednesday once again hit out at Pakistan over its continued use of crossborder terrorism, accusing Islamabad of employing terrorism as a "tool of state policy". New Delhi also reiterated that Jammu and Kashmir "has always been, is, and will remain" an integral and inalienable part of India.The remarks were made by India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish, during the High-Level Open Debate of the UN Security Council on "Natural Resources Governance: the Foundation of Peace, Security and Prosperity."Responding to Pakistan's intervention at the debate, Harish accused Islamabad of "misusing" the forum to "peddle its false narrative.""It was not our intention to bring other issues in this forum, but my delegation is compelled to respond to Pakistan, as it has chosen to misuse today's discussion to peddle its false narrative," the Indian envoy said."For the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir, it has always been, is, and will remain an integral and inalienable part of India. This is the constitutional and legal reality that Pakistan deliberately chooses to ignore."Harish also asserted that Pakistan's illegal occupation of Indian territory remained the only unresolved issue concerning Jammu and Kashmir."The only outstanding issue with regard to Jammu and Kashmir is the naked aggression and illegal occupation of India's sovereign territory by Pakistan."On the Indus Water Treaty , the Indian envoy reiterated New Delhi's longstanding position, saying Pakistan's support for crossborder terrorism had eroded the trust necessary for bilateral cooperation."Our position on the Indus Water Treaty remains clear and consistent. Cooperation on the basis of mutual trust and goodwill cannot be expected when crossborder terrorism is regularly deployed as an instrument of State policy."In his concluding statement at UN, Harish urged Pakistan to focus on addressing its own domestic challenges instead of targeting India."Instead of pointing fingers at India, Pakistan would serve itself and its people far better by putting its own house in order."
Article Summary: India on Wednesday once again hit out at Pakistan over its continued use of crossborder terrorism. New Delhi also reiterated that Jammu and Kashmir "has always been, is, and will remain" an integral and inalienable part of India. The remarks were made by India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish.

Article Title: Curbs on foreign funding not uniquely Indian, says govt, cites laws elsewhere ahead of bill to tweak FCRA
Article Text:
Govt cited comparable laws regulating foreign funding

NEW DELHI: Ahead of Parliament taking up the Foreign Contribution Regulation (Amendment) Bill, 2026, govt cited comparable laws regulating foreign funding, influence and lobbying in the US, UK, Australia and Canada to underline the "globally recognised" concern that "foreign money, when unregulated, can affect democratic institutions, electoral processes and public discourse".In a backgrounder on FCRA issued Wednesday, MHA said regulating foreign contributions and influence was "not a uniquely Indian approach", with leading democracies strengthening such frameworks over the past decade.The ministry cited the US Foreign Agents Registration Act (FARA), requiring registration and disclosure for those acting on behalf of a "foreign principal" in political activity or lobbying, and the UK's 2025 Foreign Influence Registration Scheme, which provides for up to two years' jail for political influence arrangements with a foreign power. It added that the EU has proposed transparency registers in member states for lobbying on behalf of non-EU govts.Rejecting the claim FCRA "bans" NGOs from receiving foreign funds, MHA said 16,200 associations were actively registered in 2024-25 and received Rs 22,963 crore in foreign contributions. It denied that FCRA targeted any religion or community, saying faith-based welfare activities remain eligible for foreign funding.On the proposed FCRA amendment allowing govt to seize assets of delisted NGOs, govt said the designated authority would manage assets created from foreign funds only after an organisation's FCRA registration lawfully ceases. Places of worship would retain their religious character, it said, while noting the provision for appeal.The ministry said restricting foreign nationals from key roles in FCRA-registered entities ensures foreign contributions are governed by persons with a verified connection to India. On mandatory Central approval for state-level FCRA investigations, it said FCRA is a central law."FCRA... ensures that foreign money enters India through a registered, accountable and disclosed channel... while enabling thousands of organisations to carry out legitimate, impactful work."
Article Summary: Foreign Contribution Regulation (Amendment) Bill, 2026 to be taken up in Parliament. Govt cites comparable laws regulating foreign funding, influence and lobbying in US, UK, Australia and Canada. MHA: Regulating foreign contributions and influence was "not a uniquely Indian approach"

Article Title: NTA runs on 24 permanent staff, 197 contractual and outsourced hands
Article Text:
NTA currently has just 24 permanent officials against 197 contractual and outsourced personnel

NEW DELHI: The National Testing Agency (NTA) — which handled over 6.6 crore candidate registrations since 2018 for competitive exams such as NEET-UG , JEE and UGC-NET — currently has just 24 permanent officials against 197 contractual and outsourced personnel, govt told Rajya Sabha Wednesday. The agency has 39 permanent posts filled through deputation. But the post-wise strength disclosed by the education ministry adds up to 24. These include the director general, two joint secretaries, eight directors across two pay levels, six joint directors and research scientists, three deputy directors, two assistant directors and two senior superintendents.Alongside them, NTA has 73 people on contract, including 44 consultants, 17 young professionals, five senior consultants, three advisers and a senior software developer. Another 124 people, including data analysts, consultants, legal associates and housekeeping staff, have been outsourced. The ministry said 16 additional senior posts — eight each at director and joint director levels — have been created.NTA has conducted more than 270 exams since 2018.
Article Summary: National Testing Agency (NTA) currently has just 24 permanent officials against 197 contractual and outsourced personnel. The agency has 39 permanent posts filled through deputation. NTA has conducted more than 270 exams since 2018 for competitive exams such as NEET-UG, JEE and UGC-NET.

Article Title: PM Modi urges youth to lead vision of Viksit Bharat
Article Text:
PM Modi suggested that efforts be made to develop an “institutional mechanism”

NEW DELHI: Underscoring the impor-tance of tackling the serious issue of drug abuse among youth, PM Narendra Modi Wednesday suggested that efforts be made to develop an "institutional mechanism" to break departmental silos and foster greater inter-departmental coordination and convergence in youth-centric initiatives.He also pitched for establishing India as a "global sports hub" by hosting major international sporting events and emphasised the need to adopt an integrated approach for development of sports in the country. He further stressed the importance of promoting technology and fostering innovation through startups in sports sector.The PM made the remarks while chairing a high-level meeting with secretaries to review progress in rural development, agriculture, social welfare and allied sectors towards Viksit Bharat goals at his residence at Lok Kalyan Marg here. He said the dream to build a 'Viksit Bharat' should become a 'Jan Andolan' (people's movement) and the youth should form the foundation of this vision."The PM shared his suggestions and his vision for future course of action. The meeting was marked by comprehensive and productive discussions across sectors, focusing on effective implementation, greater convergence and accelerated progress towards the goals of Viksit Bharat," said a statement from the PMO.During the meeting, attended by cabinet secretary, secretaries of key ministries as well as senior PMO officials, Modi highlighted that the widespread adoption of initiatives such as PM-KUSUM scheme by farmers can bring transformative changes to the agriculture sector and stressed the need for global mapping of skill requirements to ensure better alignment with international opportunities and future needs.PM-KUSUM scheme is one of the largest initiatives in the world to provide clean energy to more than 35 lakh farmers by solarising their agriculture pumps. The scheme also has direct employment generation potential.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi said the dream to build a 'Viksit Bharat' should become a 'Jan Andolan' (people's movement) He also pitched for establishing India as a "global sports hub" by hosting major international sporting events. He stressed the importance of promoting technology and fostering innovation through startups in sports sector.

Article Title: SC to examine plea to ease green-cracker ban
Article Text: NEW DELHI: That firecrackers cause mayhem in Delhi is not in doubt. What is less certain is whether the same yardstick of pollution can be applied to the rest of India. Things haven't been helped by the fact that different govt agencies are not on the same page when it comes to allowing production and sale of new-generation crackers. Keeping all this in mind, SC Wednesday agreed to examine a plea for relaxing its order on the sale of green crackers in the country.Hearing an application filed by a cracker manufacturer association, SC said a balance has to be maintained between competing interests of controlling pollution and employment of lakhs of workers in the industry. Additional solicitor general Aishwarya Bhati said the entire country could not be seen from the prism of Delhi, which faces a pollution crisis that is peculiar to the city. SC agreed with her view and asked all parties to come up with a solution that keeps in mind competing interests.
Article Summary: SC agrees to examine a plea for relaxing its order on the sale of green crackers in the country. SC said a balance has to be maintained between competing interests of controlling pollution and employment of lakhs of workers in the industry. Additional solicitor general Aishwarya Bhati said the entire country could not be seen from the prism of Delhi.

Article Title: Anurag Jain named new Niti Aayog CEO
Article Text:
NITI Aayog names new CEO - Anurag Jain

NEW DELHI: Govt Wednesday appointed 1989-batch IAS officer Anurag Jain, currently chief secretary of Madhya Pradesh, as chief executive officer (CEO) of Niti Aayog for two years. Centre's premier think tank has been without a full-time CEO since BVR Subramanyan's term ended last Feb.Jain comes with varied experience across ministries, having worked in department of financial services at the time of the launch of PM Jan Dhan Scheme over a decade ago. After that he moved to the PM's Office, before taking charge as DDA vice-chairperson. He had stints in the department for the promotion of industry and internal trade where he worked on PM Gati Shakti, using it as a key tool for holistic infrastructure development, and the transport and highways ministry as secretary.He will join within months of reconstitution of Niti Aayog, which now has a new vice-chairperson and new members, barring Rajiv Gauba.
Article Summary: Anurag Jain is currently chief secretary of Madhya Pradesh. Centre's premier think tank has been without a full-time CEO since BVR Subramanyan's term ended last Feb. He will join within months of reconstitution of Niti Aayog.

Article Title: As of July, 11.4% of 7.99 lakh Waqf claims rejected after uploading on central portal
Article Text:
As many as 91,623 number of waqf properties are pending at various stages of uploading

NEW DELHI: Minority affairs minister Kiren Rijiju informed Parliament on Wednesday that the data as on July 17 shows of the over 7.99 lakh waqf properties that have been been initiated for uploading on the UMEED Central Portal–2025 since it was launched in June last year, around 6.17 lakh waqf properties have been validated and approved and 91,253 properties (11.4% out of 7.99 lakh) have been rejected during verification.As many as 91,623 number of waqf properties are pending at various stages of uploading.The minister shared this data in Lok Sabha in response to a question asked by MP Sajda Ahmed who sought to know from the minister whether the registration of Waqf properties on the UMEED portal has been completed across the country and details related to the status of properties approved and rejected.In his reply the minister shared that as per the Waqf (Amendment) Act 2025 also known as the UMEED Act, every waqf registered that existed prior to the commencement of the Act, was expected to file the details of the waqf and the property dedicated to the waqf on the portal and database, within a period of six months, with a provision for further extension of up to six months by the Waqf Tribunal on sufficient cause being shown by the mutawalli."The UMEED Central Portal-2025 is a statutory portal developed in accordance with Section 3(ka) the UMEED Act, 1995, for uploading and managing the entire lifecycle of waqf properties including registration, verification, survey by the Collector, mutation, annual account audit etc. As the statutory remedy available under the Act, further extensions were granted to the State/UT Waqf Boards by the concerned Waqf tribunals/courts after expiry of initial six months," the minister stated.He added that accordingly the ministry has facilitated by reopening the UMEED Central Portal-2025 for those State/UT Waqf Boards who have been granted further extension by the Waqf tribunals or Courts for the purpose of uploading details of existing waqf and properties dedicated to waqf.
Article Summary: As many as 91,623 number of waqf properties are pending at various stages of uploading. Minority affairs minister Kiren Rijiju informed Parliament on Wednesday that the data as on July 17 shows of the over 7.99 lakh properties that have been been initiated for uploading.

Article Title: Jaishankar flags need for fair trade, supply chain reliability in meeting with Wang Yi
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Chinese counterpart Wang Yi

External affairs minister S Jaishankar underlined the significance of fair market access, trade balance and supply chain reliability in a meeting with his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi, while welcoming recent measures to normalise Sino-Indian ties like the upgrading of the visa framework and resumption of direct flights, Kailash pilgrimage and border trade.In a meeting in Manila on the margins of ASEAN events, Jaishankar also stressed that border peace and tranquility will remain a prerequisite for normal ties. Ahead of the BRICS summit India will host in September, he thanked Wang for China's support for India's presidency. While a final confirmation from Beijing is still awaited, President Xi is likely to attend the summit.India's relations with China have improved considerably since PM Narendra Modi and Xi met in Kazan in 2024, marking the end of the border standoff in eastern Ladakh. However, for India, addressing economic friction in ties including over the asymmetrical $ 100 billion trade imbalance and the need for Indian manufacturing to reliably access Chinese raw materials remains an essential next step for broader normalisation.According to official sources, India continues to raise issues related to trade restrictions imposed by Beijing and is working with the Chinese government to ensure predictability in supply chains for trade consistent with international practices."Fair market access and a trade balance ranks high in that regard. There are also concerns about predictability of supply chains. Facilitating both official and people-to-people exchanges merit our attention. We also need to agree on the meetings of various mechanisms and platforms as per our mutual priorities," said the minister in his opening remarks, underscoring the significance of the meeting in an extremely complicated global situation.Jaishankar said that while both sides had engaged to ensure border peace since October 2024, this issue would continue to need constant attention and that relevant mechanisms in that domain had to be given full support and strong encouragement.“It is natural that as two large and important nations, and that too proximate neighbours, India and China would have their own particular interests. That is why our leaders had agreed that differences should not become disputes. It is the responsibility of diplomacy to properly manage them,” said the minister.The minister reiterated India's position that the bilateral relationship must be built around the "3 mutuals" or mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity."We believe that a stable and cooperative relationship can best be developed on the basis of mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity," said Jaishankar, adding that such a relationship can make a valuable contribution to a multi-polar Asia and a multi-polar world.The minister said that relations have been gradually normalising since the October 2024 Kazan Modi-Xi meeting and this was further affirmed when the leaders met in Tianjin last year. Modi travelled to Tianjin in August 2024 for the SCO summit.
Article Summary: S Jaishankar underlined the significance of fair market access, trade balance and supply chain reliability in a meeting with his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi. He also stressed that border peace and tranquility will remain a prerequisite for normal ties. The minister said that relations have been gradually normalising since the October 2024 Kazan Modi-Xi meeting.

Article Title: 6.7 crore saplings planted by CAPFs in last 5 years: MHA tells Parliament
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Central armed police forces (CAPFs) have planted nearly 6.7 crore saplings in the last five years as part of a countrywide plantation drive, the home ministry told the Rajya Sabha on Wednesday.Junior home minister Nityanand Rai, in reply to a written question, shared that the seven CAPFs -- CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, NSG, SSB and Assam Rifles -- also developed a total 3,212 nurseries between 2020 and 2025 and set up and dedicated 1,995 ‘vatikas’ to martyrs between 2020 and 2024.A statewide distribution of the 6.9 crore saplings planted by the Central paramilitary forces in the last five years, shows north-eastern states at the top of the table. While Manipur saw the plantation of 64.8 lakh saplings, 61.2 lakh were planted in Nagaland and 59.6 lakh in Assam.CRPF and BSF, between themselves, developed 69% of the nurseries developed by the CAPFs in the last five years, according to data shared by Rai. ITBP led in development of vatikas, having set up 763 in the period 2020 to 2024.
Article Summary: The Central armed police forces (CAPFs) have planted nearly 6.7 crore saplings in the last five years. The seven CAPFs -- CRPF, BSF, CISF, ITBP, NSG, SSB and Assam Rifles -- also developed a total 3,212 nurseries between 2020 and 2025.

Article Title: 37,164 killed on 6,358 ‘black corridors’ of NHs in 3 yrs: Road ministry
Article Text:
37,164 killed on 6,358 ‘black corridors’ of NHs in 3 yrs: Road ministry

NEW DELHI: Govt has identified 6,358 “black corridors” on NH network during 2023-25, which recorded 37,164 deaths, as per road transport ministry. The maximum such black corridors were in Tamil Nadu (958), followed by UP (671) and Karnataka (607). Among districts, Viluppuram in Tamil Nadu had a maximum of 70 such corridors.Unlike isolated black spots, these corridors are continuous stretches of NH that have recorded a higher number of fatal and grievous road crashes. The identification is expected to help authorities prioritise engineering interventions and improve road safety outcomes, officials said.The road transport ministry informed the Rajya Sabha on Wednesday that black corridors have been identified based on the available crash data from Electronic Detailed Accident Report (e-DAR). As per ministry data, Pune Rural district had 60 black corridors, followed by 52 in Purba Medinipur in West Bengal, 49 in Cuddalore in Tamil Nadu and 48 in Palakkad in Kerala.Responding to a separate question, road transport minister Nitin Gadkari informed the Upper House that govt has adopted a phased roadmap for deployment of advanced traffic management systems (ATMS) across all four-lane and above NHs and expressways.The roadmap includes establishment of local, regional, and national command and control centres for centralised monitoring, incident management, traffic analytics, and coordinated response.In a written reply to another question on highway quality, the ministry said major deficiencies in NH stretches and bridges since March 2024 have been reported for 61 projects and stretches across the country. The deficiencies included collapse and defects in reinforced soil wall, girders, concrete panel, rutting and potholes. It said that 103 contractors and agencies have been blacklisted and penalised for poor quality work.
Article Summary: Govt has identified 6,358 ‘black corridors’ on NH network during 2023-25. Tamil Nadu (958), followed by UP (671) and Karnataka (607) Viluppuram in Tamil Nadu had a maximum of 70 such corridors.

Article Title: 58% of IRCTC booking requests were automated, access denied: Railways
Article Text:
Representational image

NEW DELHI: In the past six months, nearly 58% attempts to book train tickets online were by bots, not real people, the railway ministry informed Lok Sabha Wednesday. It said that on average 888 crore requests out of 1,538 crore in a month were bots, and those were flagged as automated requests and their access to the e-ticketing system was denied.A bot is an automated software application that performs repetitive tasks over a network. It follows specific instructions to imitate human behaviour at a faster rate. In a written reply, railway minister Ashwni Vaishnaw said that such “spurious attempts” that were denied access to e-ticketing systems was the maximum in June — nearly 66% of the 1,912 crore requests by bots.“Bot manager in the system protects websites at its own cloud servers by using user behavioural analysis, bot signature/ pattern matching, and real-time risk scoring to instantly distinguish humans and bots and software,” an IRCTC official said.Vaishnaw said the train ticket reservation system is robust and highly secure, and equipped with industry-standard, state-of-the-art cybersecurity controls while highlighting measures taken by railways to safeguard the system from cyber attacks, and prevent auto-filling of forms by hacking tools to curb fraud in Tatkal ticket bookings.The minister said that multiple protective layers such as network firewalls, intrusion prevention systems, application delivery controllers and web application firewalls have been deployed to safeguard the system against cyber threats.He added that little over three crore suspicious user IDs had been deactivated and 6.2 crore had been placed under revalidation during 2025-26 and 2026-27 (up to June 30).The ministry said that 503 complaints have been lodged on the National Cyber Crime Portal for suspicious booking and 13,343 suspicious emails have been blocked in the past one-and-half years. In addition, since 2021, Railway Protection Force has arrested 22,676 touts and legal action has been taken.
Article Summary: In the past six months, nearly 58% attempts to book train tickets online were by bots, not real people. On average 888 crore requests out of 1,538 crore in a month were bots, and those were flagged as automated requests and their access to the e-ticketing system was denied.

Article Title: Punjab's 'Business Class' programme helps over 20k student-led ventures garner Rs 90 cr revenue
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Punjab's 'Business Class' programme, introduced under the state government's Sikhya Kranti initiative, has helped over 20,000 student-led ventures generate nearly Rs 90 crore in revenue within a year, Higher Education Minister Harjot Singh Bains said on Sunday.The programme, launched during the 2025-26 academic session, included a compulsory two-credit course for students pursuing B.Com, BBA, B.Tech and B.Voc courses, besides those studying in Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and polytechnic colleges.According to the government, around 95,000 students from 925 higher and technical education institutions participated in the programme.Of these, 25,693 students started businesses, while 20,241 ventures generated nearly Rs 90 crore in revenue during the first year.Bains said the initiative aims to transform students from job seekers into entrepreneurs and employment generators."Our objective is not merely to award degrees but to nurture a generation of young innovators, entrepreneurs and job creators," he said in a statement.The minister further said the results demonstrate that young people can build businesses and contribute to the state's economy when provided with the right opportunities.Under Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann's leadership, Punjab's Sikhya Kranti is preparing youth "not just for the jobs of tomorrow, but to create tomorrow's opportunities," he said.The programme was implemented across 20 universities, 494 colleges, 320 ITIs and 91 polytechnic colleges.Unlike conventional classroom teaching, the course focuses on practical entrepreneurial skills, including identifying business opportunities, market validation, business setup, digital marketing, pricing, customer acquisition, financial planning and communication.The government said nearly 57,000 students, or about 60 per cent of participants, actively engaged in practical business activities during the academic session.Students launched ventures in sectors including e-commerce, food services, digital content creation, coding, graphic design, retail, beauty services, agriculture-based enterprises and handicrafts.Among the examples cited by the government were a matcha beverage startup by a Guru Nanak Dev University student, a digital career guidance platform developed by a student of Maharaja Ranjit Singh Punjab Technical University, an online jewellery business, a mushroom cultivation enterprise, a home-based nail art venture and an online electronics store.Encouraged by the programme's performance, vice-chancellors of universities across Punjab have recommended extending the course to all undergraduate programmes, including BA and BSc, from the 2026-27 academic session, the statement said.The government said the expansion is expected to equip a larger number of students with entrepreneurial skills alongside their regular academic studies.
Article Summary: Punjab's 'Business Class' programme has helped over 20,000 student-led ventures generate nearly Rs 90 crore in revenue within a year. Higher Education Minister Harjot Singh Bains said the initiative aims to transform students from job seekers into entrepreneurs and employment generators.

Article Title: Keep students focused on studies, away from protest calls: AIU to varsities
Article Text:
AIU said prolonged protests could cause loss of academic time, disrupt preparation and create avoidable stress (representative image)

NEW DELHI: The Association of Indian Universities (AIU) has asked universities and higher education institutions to counsel students against staying away from classes to join protests over recent examination controversies, saying prolonged agitation comes at a “considerable cost” to their studies.In a letter to vice-chancellors and directors of member institutions, AIU president Vinay Kumar Pathak urged them to engage with students with empathy but ensure that their energies were not “diverted by calls to protest”.The July 21 letter comes amid continuing protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Students and other protesters have demanded examination reforms, accountability for the leak and the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.“In the wake of recent concerns surrounding the conduct of certain national-level examinations, a section of students on several campuses has been staying away from classes to take part in protests and agitations — at considerable cost to their own studies,” Pathak said.While acknowledging that students’ concerns deserved to be heard “with empathy and respect”, the AIU said prolonged protests could cause loss of academic time, disrupt preparation and create avoidable stress.“Prolonged agitation exacts its heaviest price from the students themselves — in lost academic time, disrupted preparation and avoidable stress,” the letter said.The AIU argued that this cost was unnecessary as the government was committed to resolving the concerns and had conducted the recent NEET examination in a “transparent and error-free manner”. The June 21 examination was held after the original May 3 test was cancelled following a question-paper leak.“I would, therefore, earnestly appeal to you to counsel our students — with patience, sensitivity and care for their future — to remain focused on their academic pursuits and not to allow their energies to be diverted by calls to protest,” Pathak said.The association also asked students to repose faith in the government’s efforts to strengthen the examination system rather than be influenced by “rumour and speculation”.“The Hon’ble Prime Minister has himself appealed to students and has assured the nation that all possible steps will be taken to ensure that future examinations are conducted in a completely fair, transparent and secure manner,” the letter said.Pathak asked university heads to personally engage with students, mentor them and keep campuses “calm and focused”.“I am confident that, with your personal attention and compassionate leadership, our campuses will remain calm and focused, and our students will devote their full energies to their studies and to nation-building,” he said.
Article Summary: The Association of Indian Universities (AIU) has asked universities and higher education institutions to counsel students against staying away from classes to join protests. The July 21 letter comes amid continuing protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak.

Article Title: Centre appoints 1989-batch IAS officer Anurag Jain as NITI Aayog CEO
Article Text:
NITI Aayog new CEO Anurag Jain

NEW DELHI: The Centre on Wednesday appointed Anurag Jain, a 1989-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer from the Madhya Pradesh cadre, as the new Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of NITI Aayog , the government’s apex public policy think tank.The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) has approved Jain’s appointment as CEO of NITI Aayog, effective from the date he assumes charge of the post, according to an official statement.The statement said Jain will continue in the role for two years thereafter, or until further orders, whichever is earlier.
Article Summary: Anurag Jain, a 1989-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer from the Madhya Pradesh cadre. Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has approved Jain’s appointment as CEO of NITI Aayog, effective from the date he assumes charge of the post.

Article Title: 'Dharmendra Pradhan should be shown the door': Rahul Gandhi backs students - top quotes
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi's press conference

On education system and paper leaks

"The thing that is very clear to everybody is that our education system, which was known to be one of the best education systems in the world, is today a rigged system."

"In the last 10 years, 152 paper leaks have taken place and there have been zero convictions. Zero. So somebody, or a group of people, has destroyed our education system, India's most valuable asset, destroying the lives of our students. And what does the government do? Zero convictions."

"What we do know is that 152 paper leaks have taken place over the last decade. So, approximately one a month. Every month, lakhs of students are told that all the stress they went through, they're going to have to go through again."



"We are 100% in agreement with what every single student is feeling and what every single student is doing. There must be consequences to what has been done some days ago."

On students and police action

"The question is, why is this happening to our students? What exactly have our students done? Thousands and thousands of students... To have security forces aiming weapons at them, to be beaten up, to be thrashed, to be humiliated. What have they done wrong? They're protesting peacefully."

"They're simply asking, 'Listen, as students in this country, we deserve an education system that works. We deserve an education system that is fair,' and we don't have it."

"Children have committed suicide. Our students go through immense stress. And then, at the last minute, they are told that the paper has leaked. So there is a legitimate problem. And it is now a very deep problem in this country."

On students and their families

"7.5 crore students and their families are affected by this. These are middle-class and poor families. They put their hard-earned money into this... And the best part of this is zero convictions."



"They (students) have some demands. The first demand, a completely legitimate demand, is that Dharmendra Pradhan, who has failed as an education minister and is known to be corrupt, should be relieved of his duties. It is a 100% legitimate demand. There is nothing wrong with it. He has shown India and the world that he is incapable of running this system. Exam leaks are happening one after the other."

"The second demand is that every single person who raised a finger on our students — it doesn't matter who ordered it or who executed it — should be made accountable."

"They (students) have some demands. The first demand, a completely legitimate demand, is that Dharmendra Pradhan, who has failed as an education minister and is known to be corrupt, should be relieved of his duties. It is a 100% legitimate demand. There is nothing wrong with it. He has shown India and the world that he is incapable of running this system. Exam leaks are happening one after the other." "The second demand is that every single person who raised a finger on our students — it doesn't matter who ordered it or who executed it — should be made accountable." "The third thing is that the man who is running this entire system, the man who is finally accountable for all this, should have the decency to apologise to the students for what has happened."

On education minister Dharmendra Pradhan

"Dharmendra Pradhan should be shown the door, he is incapable of holding the post."

"This gentleman, Dharmendra Pradhan, has literally destroyed India's biggest asset... Our education system has been wiped out and so we fully support these demands and we fully support every single student who's on the streets."

"The government wants to push this under the carpet, but we want that Pradhan goes, the Prime Minister apologises and the education system changes."

On student protests

"Students are protesting as India's youth have no future. There must be consequences on what has been done some days ago."



"Our education system is unaffordable. The Government of India puts Rs 1.4 lakh crore in the education budget, but students spend Rs 1.32 lakh crore on one NEET exam."

"Our education system is unaffordable. The Government of India puts Rs 1.4 lakh crore in the education budget, but students spend Rs 1.32 lakh crore on one NEET exam." "Even after you have an education, you become a doctor, you become a lawyer, you learn something, you're suddenly told that the doors that should be open to you — what your country and your government owes you, a livelihood, a job — are closed."

"Manufacturing is closed. Look around you, you'll see there are only Chinese products. Entrepreneurship is closed. Take a look at who gets the money from the banks. Two or three large companies get all the money."

"Corporate jobs — AI is destroying them, especially in the IT sector. The public sector is being privatised. The only choice left is a government job. You give them one door, close all the others, then rig that system and make it unbelievably expensive. And then you expect they won't be outside. Which world are you living in?"

NEW DELHI: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Wednesday launched a fresh attack on the Centre over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, accusing the government of failing students and demanding the removal of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Addressing a press conference, Gandhi alleged that India's education system had become a ‘rigged system’, claimed there had been ‘152 paper leaks’ over the past decade with ‘zero convictions’ and criticised the police action against students protesting over the examination controversy. He also reiterated his demand that Pradhan resign.His remarks came after police used tear gas and lathi-charged protesters during the Cockroach Janata Party's (CJP) march towards Parliament over the alleged paper leak.Here are the top quotes from Rahul Gandhi’s press conference:
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi: "In the last 10 years, 152 paper leaks have taken place and there have been zero convictions" "We are 100% in agreement with what every single student is feeling," he says. Gandhi says education minister Dharmendra Pradhan should be shown the door.

Article Title: India-China ties gradually normalising since Modi-Xi Kazan meeting: Jaishankar
Article Text:
Jaishankar with Chinesecounterpart Wang Yi

NEW DELHI: External affairs minister S Jaishankar on Wednesday said India-China ties have been steadily improving since Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Xi Jinping met in Russia's Kazan in October 2024. He also stressed that peace and tranquility along the border remain the "pre-requisite" for normal bilateral relations.Speaking to Chinese foreign minister Wang Yi in Manila on the sidelines of the Asean foreign ministers' meeting, Jaishankar also raised India's concerns over market access, trade imbalance and the predictability of supply chains as New Delhi and Beijing continue efforts to normalise ties after the prolonged eastern Ladakh military standoff, PTI reported."Since the meeting of our leaders in Kazan in October 2024, relations between India and China have been gradually normalising. This direction was further affirmed when they met in Tianjin last August," Jaishankar said.He said India believes that a stable relationship between the two neighbours must be based on "mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity.""We believe that a stable and cooperative relationship can best be developed on the basis of mutual respect, mutual interest and mutual sensitivity.Such a relationship can make a valuable contribution to a multi-polar Asia and a multi-polar world," he added.Underscoring the importance of stability along the Line of Actual Control (LAC), Jaishankar said normal ties cannot progress without peace in the border areas."Peace and tranquility in the border areas is obviously the pre-requisite for normal ties. Since October 2024, both sides have engaged to ensure that important objective," he said."This will continue to need our constant attention. Relevant mechanisms in this domain must be given full support and strong encouragement," he added.Jaishankar also reiterated that differences between the two countries should not be allowed to turn into disputes."It is natural that as two large and important nations, and that too proximate neighbours, India and China would have their own particular interests. That is why our leaders had agreed that differences should not become disputes. It is the responsibility of diplomacy to properly manage them," he said.The external affairs minister said India expects progress on long-pending economic issues, including fair market access and trade."Fair market access and a trade balance ranks high in that regard. There are also concerns about predictability of supply chains. Facilitating both official and people-to-people exchanges merit our attention," Jaishankar said."We also need to agree on the meetings of various mechanisms and platforms as per our mutual priorities," he added.Jaishankar welcomed the confidence-building measures taken over the past several months, including the resumption of direct flights, the easing of visa rules, the restart of the Kailash Manasarovar Yatra and the reopening of border trade.He also thanked China for supporting India's initiatives during its Brics chairmanship. "India is the chair of the Brics this year. Let me take the opportunity to appreciate the support that China has given to various Brics activities and initiatives during our chairship," he said.
Article Summary: S Jaishankar said India-China ties have been steadily improving since PM Modi and President Xi Jinping met in Russia's Kazan in October 2024. He stressed that peace and tranquility along the border remain the "pre-requisite" for normal bilateral relations. Jaishanksar also raised India's concerns over market access, trade imbalance and the predictability of supply chains.

Article Title: List of 16 Delhi Metro stations shut again over 'security reasons'
Article Text:
Delhi Metro Rail Corporation suspended entry and exit at 16 stations today

Rajiv Chowk

Barakhambha Road

Supreme Court

Seva Teerth

ITO, Delhi Gate
