NEW DELHI: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) on Thursday suspended entry and exit at 16 metro stations across the national capital from 7.30am, claiming ‘security reasons’.In a post on X, DMRC said the decision was taken due to security concerns.While passengers will not be able to enter or exit at the affected stations, interchange facilities will continue to remain operational at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat.The stations closed for entry and exit are:The closures come as security has been intensified across central Delhi following the ongoing agitation led by activist Sonam Wangchuk and the CJP over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination and demands for accountability in the education system.Large numbers of police and paramilitary personnel have been deployed around Parliament, Jantar Mantar and other sensitive locations.Authorities have also enforced prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) across the New Delhi district.The CJP protest entered another day on Thursday, with supporters continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar after Sunday’s “Sansad Chalo” march witnessed clashes between protesters and police.DMRC has advised commuters to plan their journeys accordingly and said normal services at the affected stations will resume only after further instructions.
Article Summary: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) on Thursday suspended entry and exit at 16 metro stations across the national capital from 7.30am. The closures come as security has been intensified across central Delhi following the ongoing agitation led by activist Sonam Wangchuk.

Article Title: Mumbai-based actress ran extortion racket for many years: Andhra Pradesh CID
Article Text:
The CID has alleged that Jethwani carried out a years-long extortion scheme targeting Vijayawada businessman Kukkala Venkata Rama Vidyasagar through threats of false criminal cases, intimidation and deception

Trail of extortion and deception

HYDERABAD: A criminal case that led to the suspension of three IPS officers and disciplinary action against several Andhra Pradesh police personnel has taken a dramatic turn, with the state Crime Investigation Department (CID) naming Mumbai-based actress Kadambari Jethwani as accused No. 1 in a revised chargesheet filed before a Vijayawada court.The CID has alleged that Jethwani carried out a years-long extortion scheme targeting Vijayawada businessman Kukkala Venkata Rama Vidyasagar through threats of false criminal cases, intimidation and deception.The development marks a reversal in one of Andhra Pradesh’s most high-profile criminal cases. Vidyasagar had filed a complaint against Jethwani in Feb 2024, alleging extortion over several years.During subsequent investigation, Jethwani accused Andhra Pradesh police of illegally detaining and harassing her, triggering the suspension of three IPS officers and action against several police personnel.After the investigation was transferred to the CID following the change of govt in the state in 2024, the agency concluded that evidence supported Vidyasagar’s complaint and filed the revised chargesheet against Jethwani.According to the chargesheet, Vidyasagar first met Jethwani at a social function in Hyderabad in Nov 2009. She allegedly introduced herself as ‘Natasha‘, claimed to have won a beauty pageant and expressed an ambition to become a doctor.Investigators said their acquaintance later developed into a relationship, during which she sought financial assistance for education and personal difficulties.The chargesheet states that between 2009 and 2012, Vidyasagar paid for her educational and personal expenses, including an alleged payment of Rs 80,000 in Mumbai in March 2012.The alleged coercion began in May 2013, according to the CID, when Jethwani allegedly demanded Rs 10 lakh in a Mumbai hotel room and threatened to circulate intimate photographs and implicate him in a false rape case if he refused to pay.The chargesheet states that after Vidyasagar threatened to approach her parents, her mother Asha Jethwani, an RBI employee in Mumbai, spoke to him on the phone and allegedly suggested that the “matter be settled” by agreeing on an amount.The chargesheet further alleges that Jethwani’s father Narendra Kumar Jethwani suggested that the payment be made in instalments through family accounts.Vidyasagar allegedly transferred Rs 1 lakh to Kadambari’s account on May 14, 2013, fearing damage to his reputation and family life. The CID alleges that Jethwani and her mother later threatened to approach his wife and in-laws unless payments continued.In Feb 2015, Jethwani’s brother Ambarish Jethwani allegedly transferred Rs 1.26 lakh into Vidyasagar’s account and asked him to withdraw and return it in cash, according to the chargesheet. A month later, Jethwani allegedly demanded another Rs 10 lakh, threatening a false rape complaint.Vidyasagar claims he paid around Rs 4.2 lakh in cash instalments. Between June 2016 and Jan 2017, he transferred Rs 3.48 lakh in multiple instalments to a joint account held by Jethwani and her mother, the chargesheet states.The CID alleges that the threats continued in 2018, when Jethwani allegedly visited Vidyasagar’s residence in Andhra Pradesh and demanded Rs 2 lakh, threatening to accuse him publicly of rape if he did not pay.He allegedly paid Rs 1 lakh in cash. After blocking her phone numbers for some time, Vidyasagar allegedly received contact from Jethwani again in Sep 2023.The chargesheet states that she demanded Rs 1 crore, claiming she needed the money to establish an art gallery. It also alleges that she asked him to arrange a luxury hotel stay in Varanasi during her visit to the Kashi Vishwanath temple.During a Zoom call around the same period, Jethwani allegedly claimed her brother had become a Dubai-based real estate businessman with underworld connections and could have Vidyasagar killed if he stopped paying.The complainant allegedly arranged her travel and paid Rs 1.5 lakh in cash to her mother as an advance towards the alleged demand.The CID said its investigation relied on bank records, cheque details, hotel bookings, call data records, SMS exchanges, electronic communications, and witness statements.It told the court that scrutiny of Vidyasagar’s accounts revealed a trail of approximately Rs 1.32 crore in withdrawals and transfers linked to the alleged demands.Investigators also noted the absence of loan agreements or other documents, indicating that the payments were genuine financial transactions.While the chargesheet refers to the alleged involvement or presence of Jethwani‘s parents and brother during several incidents, the CID said it found insufficient evidence to prosecute Asha Jethwani, Narendra Kumar Jethwani, and Ambarish Jethwani.The agency has named only Kadambari Jethwani as an accused and said a supplementary chargesheet would be filed if further evidence emerges.
Article Summary: Mumbai-based actress Kadambari Jethwani is accused No. 1 in a revised chargesheet filed before a Vijayawada court. The development marks a reversal in one of Andhra Pradesh’s most high-profile criminal cases. Vidyasagar had filed a complaint against Jeth wani in Feb 2024, alleging extortion over several years.

Article Title: Indian trucker with legal California driver's licence arrested over $3 million stolen powder
Article Text:
Indian trucker Deepak Kumar. (Photo: Greenfield Police Dept)

Truck tracked using camera system

California licensing policy draws criticism

An Indian national is in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) after authorities in Pennsylvania accused him of transporting nearly $3 million worth of stolen tungsten oxide powder, The New York Post reported.The suspect, identified as Deepak Kumar , 31, of Fresno, California, was arrested after investigators tracked a truck carrying 10 pallets of the industrial material, which authorities said belonged to Mitsubishi Materials Corporation.The shipment was scheduled to be picked up on June 24, but was delayed by a day. Company officials alerted police after they were unable to contact the driver on June 26.According to The New York Post, investigators located the truck the following day with the help of a Flock camera system.According to court records, Kumar allegedly presented officers with three forms of identification — an invalid work visa, a fraudulent New York state driver's licence and a valid California driver's licence.He was arrested on theft-related charges before being transferred to McFarland, California, where he remains in ICE custody.Authorities said the stolen tungsten oxide powder was valued at $2,857,500. The material is used in products including armour-piercing ammunition, ceramics, lasers, optical equipment and high-tech window coatings.The arrest has renewed scrutiny of California's policy allowing individuals with federally issued Employment Authorization Documents (EADs) to obtain commercial driver's licences.According to the New York Post, critics argue that the policy has created a loophole by enabling some undocumented immigrants with temporary federal work authorisation to legally obtain commercial driving licences.Supporters of the policy, however, have maintained that eligibility is based on valid federal employment authorisation rather than immigration status alone.
Article Summary: Deepak Kumar, 31, of Fresno, California, was arrested after investigators tracked a truck carrying 10 pallets of the industrial material. Authorities said the stolen tungsten oxide powder was valued at $2,857,500. The material is used in products including armour-piercing ammunition, ceramics, lasers and optical equipment.

Article Title: Who is Amish Shah? Indian-origin doctor wins Arizona democratic primary
Article Text:
Amish Shah wins key democratic primary

Who is Amish Shah?



Indian-origin former Arizona state lawmaker Amish Shah won the Democratic primary for Arizona's 1st Congressional District on Wednesday.He will now face Republican Jay Feely in the November general election in a closely watched race that could help determine control of the US House of Representatives.Shah's victory over Republican-turned-Democrat Marlene Galán-Woods marks another setback for the national Democratic Party. The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) has faced scrutiny after its preferred candidates were also defeated in primaries in Maine's 2nd Congressional District and California's 22nd Congressional District.Arizona's 1st Congressional District, which includes Scottsdale and surrounding northeastern suburbs, is considered one of the most competitive House battlegrounds in the country.The seat is currently held by Republican David Schweikert, who chose to run for governor instead of seeking re-election but lost the GOP primary on Tuesday to Representative Andy Biggs. Schweikert has represented the district since 2023, after previously serving Arizona's 5th and 6th Congressional districts.Amish Shah, a physician and former Arizona state lawmaker, was born and raised in Chicago to parents who immigrated from India in the 1960s while pursuing engineering studies in the United States.Shah earned a bachelor's degree in economics from Northwestern University, before completing his medical degree through the university's Honors Program in Medical Education. He later worked at international strategy consulting firm LEK Consulting and went on to earn a Master of Public Health from the University of California, Berkeley, on a full merit scholarship.He completed his residency in emergency medicine at one of New York City's busiest Level I trauma centres and won the American Academy of Emergency Medicine's National Resident Research Competition for developing a new method of treating critically ill patients.Shah served as a faculty member and academic researcher at Mount Sinai Medical Center in New York, where he taught medical students and residents and published research in peer-reviewed journals. He also led the New York Jets' Airway Management Team, helping strengthen the NFL's emergency response protocols through research on managing catastrophic injuries, which he later presented to the NFL Physicians' Society.Shah completed a sports medicine fellowship at the University of Arizona, where his clinical work and research focused on ultrasound-guided diagnostics and procedures. He is board-certified in emergency medicine and sports medicine and currently practises across Arizona, treating both adults and children.Beyond medicine, Shah founded the Arizona Vegetarian Food Festival, a philanthropic initiative aimed at promoting healthy eating and preventing lifestyle-related diseases.Outside his medical practice, Shah enjoys playing basketball, flying small aircraft and adventure travel. He is also fluent in Gujarati and speaks conversational Spanish.
Article Summary: Amish Shah, a physician and former Arizona state lawmaker, was born and raised in Chicago to parents who immigrated from India in the 1960s. He is board- certified in emergency medicine and sports medicine and currently practises across Arizona. Shah founded the Arizona Vegetarian Food Festival, a philanthropic initiative aimed at promoting healthy eating and preventing lifestyle-related diseases.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Che Guevara: 'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on Earth' and how modern economic systems treat people like
Article Text:
'The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth'

"The life of a single human being is worth millions of times more than all the property of the richest man on earth".

The feeling that economic systems treat people like numbers on a spreadsheet.

A doctor’s speech in Havana

On Revolutionary Medicine

Placing people above property

uman beings have basic dignity that money cannot buy.

Focus on Property:

Focus on Life:

Real-world relevance today

Building a world that treats every human life as something beyond price.

When a single human life is weighed against material wealth, the right choice seems clear. Yet everyday real-world systems constantly do the math in reverse. Car companies weigh the cost of fixing a dangerous defect against the cost of paying out lawsuits for accidents. Investment firms buy up empty apartment buildings while unhoused people sleep on the streets outside. Modern society often measures value by what can be bought, sold, or protected behind gates.Against this financial way of thinking stands a bold statement:This quote rejects the idea of putting a price tag on people. It says that human life is so valuable that comparing it to money or property is a major moral mistake. The statement still matters today because it speaks to a common frustration:These words were spoken by Ernesto "Che" Guevara in Havana, Cuba, on August 19, 1960. Before he became a famous guerrilla leader in the Cuban Revolution, Guevara was a trained medical doctor.Guevara gave this speech, titled, to health workers and militia members. Cuba was undergoing massive political changes, and its healthcare system was struggling after hundreds of private doctors left the country.Guevara spoke to the remaining medical staff as a fellow doctor. He recalled his younger days traveling across South America as a medical student. On those trips, he saw poor families, untreated diseases, and children dying simply because their parents could not afford basic medicine.He explained how his experiences in the mountains during the revolution changed his thinking. Seeing poor farmworkers suffer from preventable illnesses taught him that doctoring could not just be about making money. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The quote was not just an abstract idea; it was a clear instruction to health workers rebuilding a country's medical system.At its heart, the quote pushes a simple moral idea: HThe 18th-century philosopher Immanuel Kant famously wrote that everything in the world has either a price or a dignity. Things with a price can be replaced by money, but human beings have dignity, which cannot be replaced by anything.Guevara took this philosophical point and applied it to real-world politics and economics. The quote highlights two very different ways of viewing the world:Measures value through money, ownership, and land titles.Argues that human life and health carry an absolute value that no amount of physical property can equal.By placing one single life above "all the property of the richest man on earth," the quote sets up a clear rule. It insists that property rights are man-made laws meant to serve people. The moment property rules harm human health or survival, those rules lose their moral authority.The conflict between property rights and human survival remains a major issue across modern society.Look at global healthcare. During health crises, big drug companies closely guard their patents and formulas to protect their profits. Lower-income countries often face severe shortages of life-saving medicine because making cheaper versions is blocked by international trade laws. In these cases, financial ownership is given priority over human survival.Look also at housing in major cities. Local governments frequently deal with severe homelessness while thousands of investor-owned luxury apartments sit completely empty. When laws protect empty property assets while making it illegal for homeless people to sleep on public park benches, society acts as if property claims matter more than basic human shelter.The same principle applies to technology and corporate management. Social media companies often weigh user safety against corporate profit. They decide how much effort to spend on protecting users based on how it affects their earnings. When a platform chooses to design algorithms that keep people addicted at the expense of their mental health, it makes the exact choice Guevara spoke against.Putting this quote into practice means changing how institutions run. It requires companies, courts, and governments to make decisions where human safety and health always come first, no matter the financial cost.In 1960, Cuba used Guevara's ideas to build a free public healthcare system. Despite severe economic troubles and shortages, the nation prioritized free medical care and sent doctors to help during disasters around the world. It was a direct attempt to put these words into real practice.Whether in public health, city planning, or business, the core lesson remains unchanged:
Article Summary: Ernesto "Che" Guevara gave a speech in Havana, Cuba, on August 19, 1960. He argued that a doctor’s real duty was to help build a society where no person's life was treated as worthless. The statement still matters today because it speaks to a common frustration.

Article Title: Google made history on July 22, with more profit in three months than 459 of the Fortune 500 companies generate in top line revenue in a full year
Article Text:
Google booked $99 billion in “other income”

Google-parent Alphabet has become the first company in the world to report more than $100 billion in quarterly profit, setting a new corporate record. In its second-quarter earnings released on July 22, the company posted a net income of $112.1 billion, nearly four times higher than a year ago. According to Fortune, the figure is more profit in three months than 459 Fortune 500 companies generate in total annual revenue. Helped by an 82% jump in Google Cloud revenue, Alphabet’s revenue rose 24% year over year to $119.8 billion. The record earnings were also driven by massive gains from the company's investments in artificial intelligence firm Anthropic and Elon Musk’s SpaceX.Google CEO Sundar Pichai said the results show that the company's "full stack approach to AI is delivering real, measurable value."A footnote in Alphabet’s Q2 earnings release revealed that a large part of the record profit came from $99 billion in "other income", which included “unrealized and realized gains” from Alphabet's investment portfolio. According to the company's earnings release, these gains added $77.1 billion to its net income after taxes.Much of these investment gain came from Alphabet's stakes in Claude-maker Anthropic and Elon Musk-owned space company SpaceX, both of which saw their valuations rise sharply during the quarter.Alphabet has invested $300 million in Anthropic in 2023, and that investment has since grown significantly. The companies also have a cloud partnership under which Anthropic has agreed to buy large amounts of computing capacity from Google Cloud, supporting the cloud business's rapid growth.Similarly, Alphabet owns a roughly 6% stake in SpaceX that went public last month at a valuation of $1.77 trillion. These paper gains helped bolster Alphabet’s investment portfolio and flowed through to its bottom line.Despite the record earnings, Alphabet shares fell about 3% in after-hours trading. Investors remained concerned about the company's rising spending on AI infrastructure and increasing competition for its Gemini family of AI models.
Article Summary: Alphabet posted a net income of $112.1 billion, nearly four times higher than a year ago. The figure is more profit in three months than 459 Fortune 500 companies generate in total annual revenue. The record earnings were driven by massive gains from the company's investments in artificial intelligence firm Anthropic and Elon Musk’s SpaceX.

Article Title: US government just told Harvard, Stanford, MIT and other top universities that more than you individual scientists can 'help' America beat China
Article Text:
What this may mean for Harvard, Stanford, MIT and other US universities

4 key recommendations made in the federal report

Revitalize America’s Science and Technology Enterprise



Secure U.S. Dominance in Critical and Emerging Technologies



Launch a New Golden Age Powered by AI for Science



Ensure That Science and Technology Better the Lives of All Americans



The US government plans to give money to individual scientists directly, instead of large universities and established institutions like Harvard, Stanford and MIT for science and research, especially in the AI era. The Trump administration has sent a memo to federal departments and agencies, outlining the roadmap to revitalize American research and development (R&D). According to a White House press release, the overhaul plan was laid out in a report titled ‘Science: A New Golden Age’ published by Assistant to the President and Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) Michael Kratsios. The directives will help shape a roughly $200 billion annual federal research and development budget across the government for the rest of President Donald Trump’s second term.In both report and memo, the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy argues that individual scientists can accelerate technological discovery to defeat China. “American scientific progress was the beating heart of the 20th century. After World War II, we adapted to a new world by reinventing our scientific institutions, and we must do so again today. This report lays a policy foundation that frees American scientists to do their most groundbreaking work, revives the national pursuit of ambitious scientific missions, and positions the United States to lead the AI-driven scientific revolution that will define the next century,” said Director Kratsios.The Trump administration said it wants to give more support to programs that fund individual researchers instead of colleges and universities. The report further points to programs such as the National Science Foundation's graduate fellowships and the National Institutes of Health's awards for innovative scientists as examples to follow. It also says that some universities could face challenges under a proposed Office of Management and Budget rule. The proposal would give political appointees more control over research grants and make sure funding matches the administration's priorities.As per a Wall Street Journal report, the new guidance may hurt large universities that rely on federal research funding.The White House has given federal R&D agencies “to submit an action plan within 90 days outlining implementation of these priority practices, while incorporating the budget guidance into their FY2028 submissions to the Office of Management and Budget”.The report organizes its recommendations to government, industry, academia, and philanthropy around four key pillars:The R&D enterprise should refocus support on individual scientists rather than legacy institutions, diversify funding mechanisms beyond slow consensus peer review, create agile new research organizations such as X-Labs and expanded ARPA-like entities, and establish metascience units to institutionalize continuous improvement.Federal R&D should prioritize national mission-driven scientific initiatives that unite government, industry, academia, and philanthropy around ambitious goals. These include the Genesis Mission to double the productivity of American research through AI; the Quantum Computer for Application Development and Discovery Science (QC-ADDS); commercial fusion power demonstration by the mid-2030s; crewed lunar return and lunar base development; and next-generation semiconductor technologies.The Federal government should fully fund and scale the Genesis Mission as the flagship national AI-for-science initiative to dramatically accelerate discovery; develop domain-specific scientific foundation models and high-value datasets, invest in AI-enabled verification infrastructure and autonomous laboratories; and begin transitioning toward AI-native scientific institutions better suited to an era of machine-augmented research.Federal agencies should integrate hands-on technical training and apprenticeships into STEM education, open scientific careers to skilled craftspeople and practitioners, build dense regional innovation clusters that link research with advanced manufacturing, and ensure that the economic returns of discovery reach every region and community in the country.
Article Summary: The US government plans to give money to individual scientists directly, instead of large universities and established institutions like Harvard, Stanford and MIT. The Trump administration has sent a memo to federal departments and agencies, outlining the roadmap to revitalize American research and development (R&D) The report organizes its recommendations to government, industry, academia, and philanthropy around four key pillars.

Article Title: Iran war risk: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns of oil shocks, sticky inflation and higher interest rates
Article Text: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has warned that the ongoing war in Iran could trigger oil and commodity price shocks, keeping inflation elevated and pushing interest rates higher than current market expectations, Reuters reported.The warning came in his annual letter to shareholders, a day after US President Donald Trump escalated pressure on Iran by threatening to target key infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened."Now, because of the war in Iran, we additionally face the potential for significant ongoing oil and commodity price shocks, along with the reshaping of global supply chains, which may lead to stickier inflation and ultimately higher interest rates than markets currently expect," Dimon said, news agency Reuters quoted as saying.Dimon, who has led JPMorgan for two decades, also highlighted broader geopolitical risks, including the war in Ukraine, tensions in the Middle East and friction with China."The challenges we all face are significant," he added.He said it remains uncertain whether the Iran war will achieve US objectives, while warning that nuclear proliferation remains the biggest risk linked to Iran.Markets have already begun pricing in these risks, with expectations of interest rate cuts this year largely fading amid rising inflation concerns triggered by the conflict.Last week, the benchmark S&P 500 index recorded its worst quarterly performance since 2022, weighed down by rising energy prices and geopolitical uncertainty since late February.Dimon noted that the US economy remains resilient, with consumers continuing to earn and spend, and businesses staying broadly healthy, though signs of weakening have emerged.He cautioned that economic strength has been supported by significant government deficit spending and past stimulus, while infrastructure investment needs continue to grow.At the same time, he pointed to positives such as fiscal stimulus under President Trump's "Big, Beautiful Bill", deregulation policies and rising capital expenditure driven by artificial intelligence.On financial stability, Dimon said the $1.8-trillion private credit market "probably" does not pose a systemic risk, despite investor concerns and recent withdrawals from such funds.However, he warned that in a downturn, losses across leveraged lending could exceed expectations as credit standards have weakened.Private credit markets also lack transparency and rigorous valuation benchmarks, increasing the risk of investor exits if conditions worsen, he said.Separately, Dimon criticised revised US capital rules, including Basel III and GSIB surcharge norms, calling aspects of the proposals "nonsensical" and "very flawed".He said JPMorgan’s GSIB surcharge would fall only to 5.0%, a level he described as “absurd” and “un-American”, arguing it penalises the bank’s scale and performance.
Article Summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warns Iran war could trigger oil and commodity price shocks. He also highlighted broader geopolitical risks, including the war in Ukraine, tensions in the Middle East and friction with China. Dimon criticised revised US capital rules, including Basel III and GSIB surcharge norms, calling aspects of the proposals "nonsensical"

Article Title: How selling Alaska in 1867 was a costly mistake for Russia
Article Text:
Image: Wikipedia

Why Russia agreed to the Alaska purchase

The hidden wealth Russia gave away

Strategic and geopolitical consequences







In 1867, Russia’s decision to sell Alaska to America turned out to be a historic mistake that was to go unnoticed for years to come. The sum paid by the US was only $7,200,000. Although, in the light of the circumstances at the time, it appeared logical, retrospectively, the act looks extremely shortsighted. Russia found itself economically constrained, geographically far away from the region and fearful of its falling into the hands of the British without any compensation. In reality, however, the region had proved immensely valuable both strategically and naturally. Its vast reserves of oil, gold, and natural resources would later transform it into one of the most economically significant territories in the United States.The choice of selling Alaska was based on economic and political reasons. By the middle of the 19th century, the Russian Empire experienced financial difficulties as a result of the expensive Crimean War. It had become increasingly hard for the country to manage such remote and thinly populated areas as Alaska.According to EBSCO , “the Russian-American Company was in decline, and the colony failed to yield any profit”.In addition to this, the lucrative fur trade had declined, making the territory much less economically valuable for the empire.At the same time, Russia was afraid that Britain might capture Alaska in case another war broke out between the two countries. Selling the colony to the friendly United States appeared as a logical step. As the Office of the Historian states, William H. Seward, U.S. Secretary of State, “it was a chance to expand its influence in North America and hinder further growth of Britain”.For Russia, the transaction was a chance to minimise the losses. However, it greatly undervalued the potential of Alaska.Another thing that Russia did not expect was the amount of riches that Alaska possessed. Within several decades after the acquisition, the state saw discoveries of enormous deposits of gold, oil, and other minerals.“Alaska has produced more than 40 million ounces of gold,” according to the US Geological Survey. Furthermore, Alaska is home to some of the world's most significant undeveloped mineral deposits, according to the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) . But even more importantly, the state's oil deposits turned out to be extremely valuable. The Prudhoe Bay oil field was discovered in 1968 and became one of North America's biggest.In retrospect, the amount paid by the Americans, $7.2 million, or two cents per acre, was rather laughable. According to Howard I. Kushner , the deal “robbed Russia of an enormously rich territory, the true value of which would only be recognised in the twentieth century.”In addition to resources, there is the question of strategy and how important Alaska is to the United States from a strategic standpoint. This is a very good place when it comes to defence strategy. It borders on the Arctic Ocean and also happens to be close to Russia.During the period of the Cold War, the Alaskan region became a frontier area for the United States, becoming home to military facilities and warning systems.According to Col. Michael J. Forsyth, U.S. Army, the closeness of Russia and Alaska, only about ninety kilometres apart across the Bering Strait, meant that this region became highly significant to the United States' defence plans.From today's point of view, the strategic location of Alaska makes the state very important to the policies of the U.S. related to the Arctic, energy security, and even environmental policies.Thinking back, what seems clear to us now is that the Russians sold Alaska due to immediate needs rather than future considerations. In order to solve the problem, they lost a valuable resource for Russia.Conclusively, the Alaska Purchase should not have been done as it has had a significant impact that will remain throughout history.
Article Summary: Russia sold Alaska to the U.S. in 1867 for only $7,200,000. The sale was based on economic and political reasons. However, the region proved immensely valuable both strategically and naturally. Alaska is home to some of the world's most significant undeveloped mineral deposits. The Prudhoe Bay oil field was discovered in 1968.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: BMC removes 2,499 metric tonnes of solid waste from Mumbai beaches in special cleanliness drive from July 9-19
Article Text:
The BMC headquarters

Mumbai:

The BMC collected and scientifically disposed of around 2,499 metric tonnes of solid waste, including plastic, thermocol, wooden debris, and aquatic plants, from the city’s beaches during a special cleanliness drive conducted from July 9 and 19.As high tides with waves higher than 4.5 metres were expected between July 14 and 18, this special cleanliness campaign was planned keeping in view the possibility of large quantities of garbage being washed onto the beaches.Deputy municipal commissioner (solid waste management) Kiran Dighavkar said about 34 metric tonnes were collected from the 1.1km Girgaum Chowpatty, 152 metric tonnes from the 5km Dadar-Mahim beach, 127.2 metric tonnes from the 3.5km Chimbai-Waringpada beach, 44 metric tonnes from the 8km Madh-Marve beach, 1,825 metric tonnes from the 6km long Juhu beach, 277.6 metric tonnes from the 4.5km Versova beach, and 38.82 metric tonnes from the 7km Gorai beach.
Article Summary: The BMC collected and scientifically disposed of around 2,499 metric tonnes of solid waste, including plastic, thermocol, wooden debris, and aquatic plants. The special cleanliness campaign was planned keeping in view the possibility of large quantities of garbage being washed onto the beaches.

Article Title: BMC plans AI-powered diagnostics, smart OPDs, and online bed allocation to overhaul civic healthcare in Mumbai
Article Text:
The reforms were recently reviewed by BMC commissioner Ashwini Bhide at a meeting of senior health officials

Mumbai:

The BMC has drawn up a three-phase roadmap to modernise its public healthcare system, with plans to introduce AI-powered diagnostic services, smart outpatient departments (OPDs), online hospital bed allocation and 24x7 dispensaries.The reforms were recently reviewed by BMC commissioner Ashwini Bhide at a meeting of senior health officials.In the first phase, the BMC plans to introduce smart digital OPDs to reduce waiting time, strengthen the hospital management information system and launch a central dashboard to monitor hospital services. A drive to fill vacant medical posts will be carried out even as insurance help desks will be set up in major civic hospitals.In the second phase, BMC will start a pilot online bed allocation system that will allow patients on waiting lists to be allotted vacant beds across civic hospitals. Pilot 24x7 dispensaries will also be started in each municipal ward. Artificial intelligence will be introduced in radiology, pathology and hospital resource management, while hospital staff will undergo training in soft skills, medical ethics and patient care.The BMC also plans to explore public-private partnerships to provide financial assistance of up to 50% to needy patients.The third phase will focus on developing quality standards through NABH accreditation, national and international quality certifications, disability-friendly healthcare facilities and wider adoption of digital health services.
Article Summary: The reforms were recently reviewed by BMC commissioner Ashwini Bhide at a meeting of senior health officials. In the first phase, the BMC plans to introduce smart digital OPDs to reduce waiting time. A drive to fill vacant medical posts will be carried out.

Article Title: Suspension hasty, says Bombay high court; BMC medical officer reinstated
Article Text:
The Bombay high court held that BMC’s May 7 suspension order was passed without following mandatory procedure under Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation Service Rules, 2015

Mumbai:

Bombay high court has quashed and set aside BMC’s suspension of a medical officer over alleged irregularities in birth certificates, observing that he was hastily suspended.Acting Chief Justice Ravindra Ghuge and Justice Gautam Ankhad, in the July 14 verdict, also stated that Dr Shailendra Gujar’s advocate Uday Warunjikar “could be well-placed in his submissions that because a former Parliamentarian proclaimed the suspension of the petitioner on May 2, the Corporation was driven to act in undue haste”. The bench was referring to former MP Kirit Somaiya.The bench held that BMC’s May 7 suspension order was passed without following mandatory procedure under Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation Service Rules, 2015. They directed Dr Gujar’s reinstatement in service with full salary during the suspension period.Dr Gujar’s petition said he was posted in L ward upto April 4, 2025, and thereafter transferred to H-East ward dispensary. While Somaiya had alleged irregularities in 87,000 birth certificates, in the Jan 10 FIR by Kurla police, there were neither any allegations against him nor was he named an accused. On May 2, Somaiya wrote to Azad Maidan police mentioning that Dr Gujar has been suspended.On May 7, BMC suspended him with retrospective effect from May 4 “in the backdrop” of Somaiya’s claim.BMC’s advocate Pooja Khandeparkar said the May 7 order was issued under Rule 4 A (suspension) and that Rule 4 (B) (preliminary enquiry) was not applicable. Refusing to accept that Rule 4 (A) is a stand-alone provision, the judges said while it gives power to suspend an employee pending disciplinary proceedings, Rule 4 (B) defines the manner in which such suspension could be effected. Both rules are “binding” on the BMC.The judges said “it is apparent from the sequence of events that an undue haste has driven” the BMC to suspend Dr Gujar on May 7. Referring to Warunjikar’s submission on Somaiya, they noted that Dr Gujar was suspended after a first chargesheet and preliminary enquiry (PE) for minor punishment was dropped on March 9, and approval was given for imposing major punishment requiring a full-fledged departmental enquiry. Dr Gujar was issued a fresh chargesheet on April 28. However, the record revealed no PE was conducted. While partly allowing Dr Gujar’s petition, the judges said the BMC can commence PE, “if the new chargesheet is to be taken to a logical end.”
Article Summary: Dr Shailendra Gujar’s petition said he was posted in L ward upto April 4, 2025, and thereafter transferred to H-East ward dispensary. While Somaiya had alleged irregularities in 87,000 birth certificates, in the Jan 10 FIR by Kurla police, there were neither any allegations against him nor was he named an accused.

Article Title: Widespread rain across Marathwada gives new lease of life to kharif crops
Article Text:
Rain in Nanded

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

After a considerable gap, Marathwada received widespread showers with the region recording 19 mm rainfall between Tuesday and Wednesday morning.The revival of monsoon in the region has relieved the farming community as many farmers were staring at the crisis of resowing for the kharif season. Among the eight districts of Marathwada, Hingoli received the maximum 39 mm rainfall, followed by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar (29 mm), Nanded ( 22 mm), Jalna ( 21 mm), Parbhani (16 mm), Dharashiv, Beed (both 10 mm) and Latur (9 mm).Many parts of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar city also reported occasional spells of light to moderate showers till Wednesday evening with 14 mm rainfall getting recorded between 8.30am and 6.30pm, as per IMD data. Ravi Kale , a farmer from Jatwada area of Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, said kharif crops were under stress until the latest showers. “While many other parts of the state were witnessing heavy showers, the rainy season was weak especially in July. There was a delay in sowing and a gap in the rainfall activity had posed worries before us. We pray for good rains in the days to come,” he said.Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar division has reported 79% sowing for kharif season by July 20 whereas Latur division has recorded 77% sowing. As per the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and surrounding areas of the region are likely to have generally cloudy skies with chances of moderate rain for at least next 48-hours.Marathwada has received 205mm rainfall since June 1, which is 77% of the expected showers.
Article Summary: The revival of monsoon in the region has relieved the farming community. Hingoli received the maximum 39 mm rainfall, followed by Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar (29 mm) Nanded ( 22 mm), Jalna ( 21 mm), Parbhani (16 mm), Dharashiv, Beed (both 10 mm) and Latur (9 mm)

Article Title: Railway minister silent on funding & timeline for Dharashiv-Sambhajinagar rail route
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

The proposed 240km railway line linking Dharashiv, Beed and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar continues to remain in limbo, with Union railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw reiterating that only survey work for the project has been sanctioned. He provided no clarity on funding or timelines.Responding to a question raised by Beed NCP (SP) MP Bajrang Sonawane in the Lok Sabha, Vaishnaw said the detailed project report (DPR) for the rail route is being prepared following the sanction of survey work.Sonawane had sought details on the release of initial funds, raised concerns over inadequate budgetary allocations and asked whether the project would receive funding in the forthcoming Union Budget.In his written reply on Wednesday, Vaishnaw did not address the funding issue directly, stating that the project's future would depend on statutory approvals and consultations.“After preparation of DPR, sanctioning of the project requires consultation with various stakeholders, including state govts and necessary approvals, viz. appraisal of NITI Aayog, Ministry of Finance etc. As sanctioning of projects is a continuous and dynamic process, exact timelines depend upon appraisals and approvals by various stakeholders,” he said.The Railway Board had sanctioned the final location survey for the Dharashiv-Beed-Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar rail route in March last year. The survey is intended to determine the final alignment of the proposed railway line and facilitate preparation of the DPR.The lack of a definitive commitment has disappointed rail activists, who have long been advocating the project. “The rail line in question has been the dream for Marathwada for more than two decades. Now with Beed getting on the rail map of India finally, the project has become more relevant and necessary,” said railway activist Raj Somani.
Article Summary: The proposed 240km railway line linking Dharashiv, Beed and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar continues to remain in limbo. Union railway minister Ashwini Vaishnaw reiterating that only survey work for the project has been sanctioned. He provided no clarity on funding or timelines.

Article Title: ‘Gross abuse of the process of law’: HC imposes Rs 10,000 costs on Jalgaon Peoples Co-operative Bank for repeat litigation
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:

Bombay high court’s Aurangabad bench has pulled up The Jalgaon Peoples Cooperative Bank Limited for re-litigating an issue already settled against it, dismissing the bank’s writ petition and imposing costs of Rs 10,000 for what it termed a “gross abuse of the process of law”.Justice Sachin Deshmukh observed that the bank had attempted to reopen a controversy on which it had already suffered adverse orders, leading to needless consumption of valuable judicial time. “The conduct of the petitioner bank in re-litigating an issue on which it has already suffered adverse orders clearly amounts to a gross abuse of the process of law. This court cannot countenance a public financial institution pursuing unnecessary litigation and costs need to be imposed to curtail such frivolous and vexatious litigation. ..” justice Deshmukh observed.The petition had challenged a Nov 11, 2013, order of the commissioner for cooperation and registrar of cooperative societies, Maharashtra, directing the bank to provide documents sought by a borrower who was also a member of the cooperative society.Court records showed the respondent had taken a term loan of Rs 1.10 crore in Nov 2006. After defaulting on repayment, recovery proceedings were initiated under the Maharashtra Cooperative Societies Act, culminating in a recovery certificate issued by the assistant registrar in Nov 2012.Subsequently, the borrower sought copies of the bank’s balance sheets, profit and loss accounts, audit reports, minutes of general body meetings and other records. The bank declined the request, arguing that the borrower’s status as a wilful defaulter suspended his membership rights.The borrower then approached the commissioner for cooperation, who directed the bank to furnish the information. Before the high court, the bank contended that a defaulter loses the right under Section 32 of the Maharashtra Cooperative Societies Act to inspect internal records.Rejecting the argument, justice Deshmukh held that Section 32 granted every member a statutory right to inspect society records and obtain certified copies on payment of prescribed fees. The court ruled that this right could not be denied merely because a member had defaulted on a loan.The court also held that the commissioner had acted within jurisdiction in directing disclosure of the requested documents, including audit reports. Significantly, the high court noted that the same bank had faced an identical issue in an earlier writ petition decided by a division bench on April 8, 2014, which had categorically upheld a member’s right to obtain certified copies of statutory records.Having already suffered an adverse ruling on the issue, the bank could not seek to revive the dispute through fresh proceedings, the court said. Further, terming the petition devoid of merit, the court dismissed it and directed the bank to deposit Rs 10,000 with the HC legal services sub-committee within four weeks.
Article Summary: Bombay high court’s Aurangabad bench has pulled up The Jalgaon Peoples Cooperative Bank Limited for re-litigating an issue already settled against it. Justice Sachin Deshmukh observed that the bank had attempted to reopen a controversy on which it had already suffered adverse orders, leading to needless consumption of valuable judicial time.

Article Title: Around 1kg fake silver jewellery found in donation box at Purushottam temple
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:

Nearly one kilogram of imitation silver jewellery was found in the donation box of the renowned Purushottam temple at Purushottampuri in Beed district after jewellers examined offerings made by the devotees during the recently concluded Adhik Maas pilgrimage.Traditionally, devotees donate precious metals at this temple during the Adhik Maas period. The scrutiny of jewellery and ornaments donated at the temple revealed that while devotees offered around 9 grams of gold and 446 grams of genuine silver jewellery, nearly 973 grams of silver-coloured imitation ornaments had also been placed in the donation box.Temple committee chairperson Vijay Golekar said the month-long pilgrimage concluded peacefully with the cooperation of the temple administration, villagers, public representatives, police, health and revenue department officials.“Devotees donated around Rs 1.5 crore to Lord Purushottam. They also offered gold and silver ornaments. However, nearly 973 grams of the silver jewellery was found to be fake during examination," he said.Those associated with the temple administration suspect that the possibility of jewellers selling silver colour polished metals by falsely claiming it to be original silver to the gullible devotees, who ended up donating it to the temple.The pilgrimage, held from May 17 to June 15 during the Adhik Maas period, witnessed an unprecedented turnout with an estimated 50 lakh devotees visiting the temple situated on the banks of the Godavari river in Majalgaon taluka.The temple is considered the country's only shrine dedicated to Lord Purushottam and is associated with the sacred Chakratirth, making a holy dip in the Godavari and darshan of the deity especially significant during Adhik Maas.According to the temple committee, devotees contributed around Rs 1.5 crore in cash during the pilgrimage, in addition to donations of gold and silver ornaments. The committee also noted a substantial rise in footfall compared to the previous Adhik Maas pilgrimage held three years ago.Temple authorities said the discovery of imitation silver ornaments came to light only after the donated jewellery was examined by authorised jewellers following the conclusion of the pilgrimage. No complaint has been registered, and the committee has not indicated whether any further action will be initiated regarding the fake jewellery donations.
Article Summary: Traditionally, devotees donate precious metals at this temple during the Adhik Maas period. The scrutiny of jewellery and ornaments donated at the temple revealed that while devotees offered around 9 grams of gold and 446 grams of genuine silver jewellery.

Article Title: Aurangabad bench of Bombay high court issues notices on plea seeking CBI, ED probe into alleged Rs 3,000 crore investment scam
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:

The Aurangabad bench of the Bombay high court on Monday issued notices on a criminal writ petition seeking transfer of the investigation into the alleged Tredz-Infinite Becon-Sispay investment scam — worth over Rs 3,000 crore — to the Central Bureau of Investigation and the Enforcement Directorate (ED).A division bench of Justices Sandipkumar More and Vaishali Patil-Jadhav sought responses from all respondents, including the Union govt, after hearing the petition.The petition has been filed by Oneness for Solution Foundation, a registered society claiming to represent around 3,000 alleged victims of the scam, along with Nitin Gangarde, the original complainant in an FIR registered at the Shrigonda police station in Ahilyanagar district.Appearing for the petitioners, advocates Ashish Sonawane and Vilas Sonawane told the court that at least eight FIRs have been registered at different police stations across Maharashtra in connection with the alleged fraud and that nearly 1.3 lakh investors are believed to have been duped.According to the petition, the accused masterminds of the massive Ponzi scam allegedly operated a vast network comprising partnership firms, LLPs, private limited companies and a multi-state credit cooperative society, promising monthly returns of 8-10% through investments in the capital market.The plea further alleges that investors were lured by using the name of Tredz Investment Private Limited, a SEBI-, NSE- and BSE-registered entity. It refers to a Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) adjudication order under which the regulator allegedly cancelled the company’s registration and imposed a penalty of Rs 1 crore after recording the alleged modus operandi.The petitioners have also questioned the manner of the ongoing investigation. They contended that while provisions of the Banning of Unregulated Deposit Schemes (BUDS) Act have been invoked in cases registered in Pune and Ahilyanagar, the investigation has not been transferred to the CBI as envisaged under the law. The plea further alleges that partial chargesheets filed in some cases indicate serious lapses in the probe.The petition additionally claims that the proceeds of the alleged fraud were laundered through banking channels and that some accused have acquired foreign citizenship or overseas residency while allegedly transferring funds abroad through offshore entities and cryptocurrencies.Seeking intervention by central agencies, the petition has urged the HC to direct an ED investigation under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), initiate action against fugitive accused under relevant laws, and hand over the overall investigation to the CBI.
Article Summary: The petition has been filed by Oneness for Solution Foundation, a registered society claiming to represent around 3,000 alleged victims of the scam. A division bench of Justices Sandipkumar More and Vaishali Patil-Jadhav sought responses from all respondents, including the Union govt, after hearing the petition.

Article Title: HC refuses to revive delayed divorce appeal after ex-husband’s remarriage
Article Text:
Bombay high court’s Aurangabad bench

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has held that once the statutory period for challenging a divorce decree expires and a former spouse legally remarries, a delayed appeal against the divorce becomes infructuous and condoning the delay serves no useful purpose.A division bench of Justice Sandipkumar More and Justice Vaishali Patil-Jadhav dismissed a woman’s plea seeking condonation of a 381-day delay in filing an appeal against a family court decree dissolving her marriage. The court also rejected the proposed appeal, holding that it had become infructuous from the outset as the woman’s former husband had contracted a valid second marriage after the limitation period for filing an appeal had lapsed.The bench observed that under Section 15 of the Hindu Marriage Act and Section 19 of the Family Courts Act, either spouse was entitled to remarry if no appeal was filed within the prescribed limitation period. Once that period expires, the divorce decree attains finality, enabling the parties to lawfully enter into another marriage, it observed.The woman had contended that she became aware of the divorce decree only during proceedings under the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act.She argued that her former husband’s subsequent remarriage could not extinguish her statutory right to appeal and sought support from principles applied in service law and the doctrine of lis pendens.Rejecting the argument, the court held that principles governing service matters and property disputes were not applicable to matrimonial litigation. “The reliance placed on principles from Service Law or the doctrine of lis pendens under Section 52 of the Transfer of Property Act are inapplicable to the facts of the present case,” the bench observed.Relying on the Supreme Court’s decision in Krishnaveni Rai vs Pankaj Rai and an earlier Bombay HC judgment, the bench held that a second marriage solemnised after the expiry of the appeal period remained valid and could not be invalidated merely because a delayed appeal was filed later. While the right to appeal was a substantive right, it remained subject to the law of limitation, the bench said.The court also found the woman’s explanation for the delay unconvincing, noting that she had participated in the divorce proceedings by filing a written statement but subsequently chose to remain absent. It further clarified that dismissal of the appeal would not bar the woman from independently pursuing a claim for permanent alimony before the appropriate forum.
Article Summary: Bombay HC’s Aurangabad bench has held that once the statutory period for challenging a divorce decree expires and a former spouse legally remarries, a delayed appeal against the divorce becomes infructuous. The court also rejected the proposed appeal, holding that it had become inf Structuous from the outset.

Article Title: 35,000-seat cricket stadium planned near Kolhapur
Article Text:
Located about 15 km from Kolhapur city, the proposed complex will be developed as a multi-sport hub rather than a cricket-only venue.

Kolhapur

:

A 12.76-hectare plot at Vikaswadi MIDC near Kagal has been transferred by the Maharashtra government to the Kolhapur District Cricket Association (KDCA) for the development of an international cricket stadium.The memorandum of understanding (MoU) was signed on Tuesday between the state sports department and the KDCA. Former Rajya Sabha MP and KDCA president Sambhajiraje Chhatrapati signed the agreement on behalf of the association, which will now be responsible for raising funds and executing the project.Located about 15 km from Kolhapur city, the proposed complex will be developed as a multi-sport hub rather than a cricket-only venue. Besides the stadium, it will include facilities for boxing, judo, archery, wrestling, swimming, table tennis, fencing and other sports. A cricket museum will also be built, with the project expected to generate employment opportunities for local residents.The cricket stadium, with a seating capacity of around 35,000, will be built in accordance with BCCI and ICC norms. The project will feature a modern pavilion, practice grounds, an indoor training centre, a sports science centre, a clubhouse, floodlights, a cricket museum, parking facilities for players, officials and spectators, internal roads, drainage systems, a solar power project and advanced security infrastructure.The demand for an international cricket stadium in Kolhapur had been pursued for years by veteran cricketers Bal Patankar and Ruturaj Ingale, who sought land for the project. In September 2024, Sambhajiraje Chhatrapati took up the proposal with then deputy chief minister Ajit Pawar , following which the government approved the transfer of land from MIDC to the KDCA.The MoU was signed on Tuesday, on the eve of Ajit Pawar’s birth anniversary, in the presence of chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar.Sambhajiraje said construction of the stadium would begin soon and that the facility would be built to international standards with an eye on the future of the sport.“The stadium will create new opportunities for thousands of aspiring cricketers in western Maharashtra and the Konkan region. Hosting national and international cricket competitions, providing high-quality training facilities, and promoting sports tourism will boost the local economy significantly. By raising the necessary funds and completing all processes transparently and with proper planning, we are determined to build a world-class cricket stadium for Kolhapur as soon as possible,” he said.Ministers Prakash Abitkar, Hasan Mushrif and Shivendraraje Bhosale, along with KDCA office-bearers, were present at the MoU signing ceremony in Mumbai.
Article Summary: A 12.76-hectare plot at Vikaswadi MIDC near Kagal has been transferred by the Maharashtra government to the Kolhapur District Cricket Association. Besides the stadium, it will include facilities for boxing, judo, archery, wrestling, swimming, table tennis, fencing and other sports.

Article Title: Fraud worth Rs 61.7 crore uncovered at Sant Balumama temple, 2 booked; probe handed over to Economic Offences Wing
Article Text:
The Balumama temple at Adamapur is a prominent pilgrimage centre in the region, drawing lakhs of devotees from Maharashtra and Karnataka

Kolhapur

:

An alleged fraud involving Rs 61.7 crore at the Sant Sadguru Shri Balumama Devalaya Trust in Adamapur, Kolhapur, has led to the registration of a criminal case against two — the trust’s former working president and secretary. The investigation has now been handed over to the Economic Offences Wing.Superintendent of police Neelotpal said the allegations include the purchase and sale of temple land using forged documents and misuse of trust funds. “Given the magnitude of the case, the probe was transferred to the Economic Offences Wing (EOW),” he said.The case was registered at the Bhudargad police station on a complaint filed by Rangrao Gadkari, an inspector from the charity commissioner’s Mumbai office. According to the complaint, multiple petitions were filed in the Bombay high court alleging financial irregularities in the management of the trust, prompting a detailed inquiry.Investigations revealed irregularities worth nearly Rs 30 crore between March 2020 and March 2026 in transactions involving trust-owned land and the valuation shown while selling Balumama’s flock of 44,000 sheep. The HC had constituted an inquiry committee to examine the allegations.Deputy superintendent of police Suvarna Patki, of the EOW said, “Important documents from the Devasthan committee have been seized, and the scrutiny of the documents is currently on. The investigation will give clarity on the matter. Strict action will be taken against those found guilty.”The Balumama temple at Adamapur is a prominent pilgrimage centre in the region, drawing lakhs of devotees from Maharashtra and Karnataka. The alleged embezzlement in the trust has triggered concern among devotees in both states.Meanwhile, the trust’s former working president denied any involvement in the alleged fraud. “I held the position of the working president of the Devasthan Trust only from May 2024 to July 4, 2025. During this tenure, there was no malpractice. I have no connection with the alleged embezzlement as I was not the working president then. Why was a case registered against only two when there were 21 trustees?” he asked.
Article Summary: Alleged fraud at Sant Sadguru Shri Balumama Devalaya Trust in Adamapur, Kolhapur. The investigation has now been handed over to the Economic Offences Wing. The alleged embezzlement in the trust has triggered concern among devotees in Maharashtra and Karnataka.

Article Title: Increase discharge from Almatti Dam, Sangli irrigation dept urges K’taka govt
Article Text:
Almatti dam is currently 79% full

Kolhapur:

TOI

The Sangli irrigation department has written to the Karnataka govt asking them to keep all gates of the Hippargi Barrage open as the flow of the Krishna river was likely to increase following heavy rainfall received in Sangli, Satara and Kolhapur districts.The department, in the letter, has stated that as Almatti dam was currently 79% full, there‘s a possibility of flooding in villages along the Krishna river in Kolhapur and Sangli districts.“The Karnataka irrigation department should immediately increase the discharge from the Almatti dam and open all gates of the Hippargi barrage on the Krishna river,” read the letter sent to the Karnataka govt on Monday.Seema Mohite, executive engineer of Sangli irrigation department, said, “As per the decision taken during the inter-state coordination meeting, the Karnataka administration has been requested to keep all the gates of Hippargi Barrage fully open so that the natural flow of Krishna river remains uninterrupted during the monsoon. It has also been demanded that the water level of Almatti dam should be maintained between 517 and 517.5 meters till mid-August. ”Currently, the water level in Almatti dam is around 518 meters with 46,848 cusecs inflowing into the dam and 12,500 cusecs being discharged. The dam has a storage capacity of 94.64 TMC and is 79% full.Retired engineer and member of Krishna Flood Control Committee Prabhakar Kengar, while talking to, said, “During the inter-state meeting, both the irrigation departments had decided to maintain the water level of the Almatti dam between 517 and 517.5 meters till mid-Aug.Yet, the water level of the Almatti dam has been kept at 518 meters on July 20 itself. This means that the Karnataka irrigation department has violated the decision taken in the meeting.”Meanwhile, on Tuesday, moderate rainfall continued in the ghat and dam catchment areas of Satara and Kolhapur districts. In Satara, Navaja received 86mm showers, Mahabaleshwar 78mm and Koyna 67mm between Monday and Tuesday.In Kolhapur, Radhanagari dam (107mm), Jambre (139mm) and Sarfnala (122mm) were among the highest rainfall receiving dam catchments on Tuesday.
Article Summary: The Sangli irrigation department has written to the Karnataka govt asking them to keep all gates of the Hippargi Barrage open. The department, in the letter, has stated that as Almatti dam was currently 79% full, there’s a possibility of flooding in villages along the Krishna river in Kolhapur and Sangli districts.
